jobs.prompt: Create a clean academic paper figure explaining the data classification scheme for RehearFit low-label AudioScan training. Use a left-to-right flow with four major blocks: Raw measurements, Split by evaluation role, Subject/ear grouping, Row-level target. Show that Inside data is used for training and subset selection, Outside holdout is used only for final evaluation. Show subject-level grouping with icons labeled subject and ear side, then group_key = subject + ear. Show each row target as a compact 12-band LTASS dB vector. Include short labels only: Inside train pool, Outside holdout, subject-based fraction, group median evaluation, row-level 12-band target. Visually emphasize no row leakage and no teacher or pseudo-label supervision. White background, sparse vector schematic, teal blue orange accents, publication-ready layout, 16:9.

Create a clean academic paper figure showing how low-label subjects are selected from the Inside train pool. Show each subject as one point in a normalized LTASS feature space built from the per-subject median 12-band row target. Use two side-by-side selection panels. Panel A: 5% highfreq_quantile, sort subjects by high-frequency score using bands 6K to 12.5K, split into quantile bins, choose center-near candidates from each bin to preserve high-frequency coverage. Panel B: 10% balanced_center, select about 70 percent from the main distribution center and the remaining subjects from low and high high-frequency tails. Add short callouts: select subjects, then keep all rows from selected subjects; seed fixed; Outside not used. Use minimal text, paper-style arrows, compact legend, white background, 16:9.

Create a clean academic mechanism figure explaining why the selected low-label data works with the current RehearFit model. Show a three-part flow: representative subject subset, AugmentDirectLateShrink model, robust Outside metrics. In the model block, show old-style LPS input, weak noise plus frequency mask augmentation, train-subset mean output initialization, shared early CNN channels 4->8->16->32, late shrink channels 16->8, compact flatten 432, direct 12-band dB regression. Add comparison badges: Direct6 609,090 params vs LateShrink 88,074 params; forward speed 2.894 vs 2.812 ms per batch; 10% median_all 1.4068 to 1.3783; 10% high-frequency median 1.7521 to 1.5678. Visual message: subject coverage stabilizes scarce labels, smaller late channels reduce FC pressure, high-frequency coverage reduces tail errors. Keep labels sparse and accurate, white background, teal blue orange accents, conference-paper style, 16:9.
